title: Local editor, command, Git, and configuration core
summary: Switching organizations leaves open buffers, local Git state, and on-disk profile files untouched. Managed projections rebind on the next managed action.
local_core_continuity: Local editor, command, Git, and configuration core work without managed identity, network reachability, seat assignment, or quota budget.

title: Extension registry and signed mirror
summary: Org switch rebinds the active mirror and any signed policy bundle to the new org; cached registry metadata is invalidated and re-resolved.
local_core_continuity: Already-installed extensions execute regardless of registry or marketplace reachability; local discovery via CLI or local bundle remains available.
absence_narrows_to: With no live registry, install narrows to mirror, file import, and CLI-attached local bundles; discovery falls back to the registry strawman index until the next mirror sync.

title: Identity envelope and signed policy distribution
summary: Switching organizations requires the new org's signed identity envelope and policy bundle. Managed actions deny until the handoff completes; local core is unaffected.
local_core_continuity: Local editing, search, Git, tasks, and local AI continue under local configuration or last-known policy when the policy service is unreachable.
absence_narrows_to: Without a live policy service, managed actions narrow to restricted-managed-only until a fresh signed bundle is imported or the grace window expires.

title: Remote workspace control plane
summary: Org switch rebinds the workspace fleet to the new org's control plane. Live sessions in the prior org freeze and export rather than transfer.
local_core_continuity: Local folders, direct SSH or container attach, and local Git remain user-controlled. Workspace orchestration loss does not block local editing.
absence_narrows_to: Without a managed control plane, work narrows to local folders and direct SSH or container attach. Managed workspace hour meters do not transfer to local work.

title: AI provider routing and policy gateway
summary: Org switch rebinds gateway routing, redaction, and audit to the new org's policy. In-flight requests complete under the prior org's policy and are not silently re-attributed.
local_core_continuity: Local and BYOK AI routes continue independent of the managed gateway. Loss of managed AI does not disable edit, search, Git, or tasks.
absence_narrows_to: With no managed gateway, AI narrows to BYOK and local model routes. Managed redaction and audit no longer apply.

title: Managed AI quota, audit, and billing surface
summary: Switching to an organization with no paid AI seat denies managed AI until a seat is assigned. The prior org's audit export remains downloadable for the grace window.
local_core_continuity: Loss of paid AI seat narrows managed AI only. Edit, search, Git, tasks, and BYOK AI remain available.
absence_narrows_to: With no paid seat, managed AI narrows to denied. BYOK and local AI routes remain available.

title: Managed settings sync
summary: Org switch rebinds sync to the new org's storage and policy. Profiles from the prior org are exportable but not silently merged into the new org.
local_core_continuity: Local profile files remain the source of truth. Sync disable or seat revocation does not mutate or remove local files.
absence_narrows_to: With no paid seat, managed sync narrows to local profile files only. Export and file-import remain available.

title: Support ingest and telemetry pipeline
summary: Org switch rebinds support ingest routing. Already-uploaded bundles stay with the prior org's retention window and are not silently re-attributed.
local_core_continuity: Local and offline support bundles can always be generated and inspected without upload.
absence_narrows_to: With no managed ingest, support narrows to local bundle generation and manual transfer.

title: Fleet admin UI and SCIM dashboards
summary: Admin handoff is required to bind the dashboard to a new org. Without admin handoff, the dashboard denies until resolved; self-hosted identity continues.
local_core_continuity: Removing admin UI access does not corrupt local clones, unsaved buffers, or already-exported policy bundles.
absence_narrows_to: Without admin UI access, fleet posture narrows to file-based signed bundles and standards-based identity.

title: Managed cloud daily-driver parity (reserved)
local_core_continuity: Local-first beta scope continues; managed-cloud daily-driver parity is not claimed.
